data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but never added to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't see it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file before `CoreCompile`, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk, but the compiler doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` items after writing it:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added inside the `GenerateSampleCode` target, after the `WriteCodeFile` task.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it. The generated file existed in `obj/` but wasn't in the `Compile` item group.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation. This works because MSBuild allows dynamic item additions inside targets that run `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"`.
